title: 10 best herbal capsules for acute bronchitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called echinacea, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways and ease congestion. The capsules also contain antioxidants that help protect the body from further damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, purple coneflower has natural decongestant properties that can help relieve coughing and shortness of breath associated with acute bronchitis, making it a valuable natural remedy for this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation in the airways, which can alleviate symptoms such as coughing and wheezing in acute bronchitis patients.

Antiviral activity: The herbs in purple coneflower capsules have been found to exhibit antiviral properties, which can help combat viral infections that often cause acute bronchitis and reduce the severity of symptoms.

Expectorant effects: Purple coneflower capsules may help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and breathlessness in patients with acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and relieve respiratory congestion associated with acute bronchitis.

Seeds: Containing a unique combination of fatty acids, phytosterols, and phenolic compounds that exhibit expectorant and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly rinse roots and flowers with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Dry roots and flowers separately using a dehydrator at 60-70 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried echinacea purpurea roots and 20 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal mixture at a dosage of one capsule taken twice daily for 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been traditionally used to soothe inflamed respiratory mucous membranes. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root help to reduce bronchial inflammation and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and relief from coughing. Additionally, licorice's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs, further alleviating symptoms associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, easing symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
Saponins: Exerts anti-inflammatory effects by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, which can help alleviate bronchial constriction and reduce mucus secretion.
Flavonoids: Possesses antioxidant properties that help protect the respiratory tract from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic bronchitis and other respiratory diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of licorice plant contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder from a trusted source.
Mix 10 grams of the powdered root with 40 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 100 milliliters of distilled water and stir until the mixture forms a gel-like paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste using a capsule filling machine or spoon and carefully fill each one.
Store the finished herbal licorice capsules in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain bioactive compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbs in these capsules, such as dandelion root and leaf, work to reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby relieving symptoms like wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, the antioxidants in these herbs help to combat oxidative stress and promote the body's natural healing processes, making it easier for the lungs to recover from infection. This natural approach can help alleviate bronchitis symptoms and support overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs, which can alleviate symptoms of bronchitis such as coughing and wheezing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: Dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots contain inulin, a prebiotic fiber that can help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits, also known as seed heads or pappi, have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots taraxacum officinale at dawn when they are most potent and dry them immediately.
Grind dried dandelion roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle after 24 hours of drying.
Mix the ground dandelion root with gelatin and water to form a paste in 30 minutes under refrigeration.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the prepared herbal paste and store them in an airtight container for up to 2 years.
Consume one capsule twice daily as needed or until symptoms of acute bronchitis subside within 1 week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The thymol present in thyme capsules helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, thymol's antibacterial properties help to combat the underlying bacterial infection causing acute bronchitis, reducing the severity of symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Thyme capsules can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath associated with bronchitis.
Antimicrobial property: Thyme capsules contain antimicrobial agents that can help combat bacterial infections that often accompany acute bronchitis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in relieving congestion and cough symptoms associated with acute bronchitis.

Barks: The barks of the thyme plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues due to their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help combat infection and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, ensuring their quality and potency for optimal results.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 24 hours to preserve its therapeutic properties.
Grind the dried thyme into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure uniform particle size and ease of dosing.
Fill empty #0 gelatin capsules with 200-400mg of the powdered thyme, depending on individual needs and desired dosage, and seal securely to maintain freshness.
Store the prepared herbal thyme capsules in a cool, dry place for up to six months, shaking well before use to ensure uniform dosing and optimal bioavailability.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain a natural compound that has been shown to help reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. The antioxidant properties of lemon balm also help to combat oxidative stress caused by the infection, promoting faster recovery. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects can soothe coughs and relieve congestion, providing relief from the discomfort associated with acute bronchitis. This natural remedy can be a safe and effective way to alleviate symptoms without relying on pharmaceutical medications.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lemon balm's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help combat the underlying viral infection that causes acute bronchitis, reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in lemon balm capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, making breathing easier and more comfortable.

Expectorant properties: Lemon balm's expectorant compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and bronchial passages, relieving coughs and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease respiratory congestion, making them effective in relieving symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant have natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from coughing and breathing difficulty.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in morning when dew still present for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation method or shade drying for 24 hours.
Grind dried lemon balm leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of ground lemon balm and seal promptly to prevent contamination.
Store prepared herbal lemon balm capsules in airtight container away from direct sunlight for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Radium weed capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs. The active compounds in radium weed, such as menthol and eucalyptus, help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier for the body to expel it. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs help to soothe irritated airway tissues and reduce coughing fits, providing quick relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the radium weed contain bioactive compounds that help to alleviate respiratory symptoms and reduce inflammation in the lungs, making them effective in treating acute bronchitis.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the radium weed have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat infections and reduce coughing in patients with acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh euphorbia peplus roots and stems, approximately 100 grams, free from dirt and debris.
Dry the collected plant material using a food dehydrator at 55 degrees celsius for 48 hours, or until brittle.
Crush the dried roots and stems into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, resulting in a 250-milligram capsule filling.
Mix 1000 milligrams of the powdered euphorbia peplus with 500 milligrams of rice flour and 50 milligrams of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two years.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Antiviral properties: Elderberry has been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, including those that cause bronchitis. This can help prevent the spread of the infection and alleviate symptoms like coughing and congestion.
Expectorant properties: Elderberry's mucilaginous compounds may help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing coughing fits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pink peony capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain the natural essence of the herb, which has been traditionally used to ease respiratory tract inflammation and congestion. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier for patients to breathe. Additionally, pink peony's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, promoting a more rapid recovery from acute bronchitis. This natural remedy has been shown to provide relief from symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pink peony's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, making it easier to breathe and relieving coughing.
Expectorant: The mucilages present in pink peony capsules can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing.
Antimicrobial: Pink peony's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause acute bronchitis, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs associated with acute bronchitis.
Rhyzomes: Contain sesquiterpenes, which have antitussive and expectorant properties that help soothe coughs and loosen mucus in the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pink peony roots paeonia officinalis in the morning after the dew has dried.
Clean and dry the harvested roots thoroughly under shade for at least 3 hours.
Cut the cleaned roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 24 hours.
Mix the powdered pink peony root with 20% of water by weight to create an extraction mixture and let it sit for 2 hours.
Fill hard-shell capsules with a dosage of 300mg of the extraction mixture per capsule and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to soothe and calm the airways. The anti-inflammatory properties of elderberry help reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, elderberry's antiviral properties help combat the underlying viral infection, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. As a result, herbal elder capsules can provide fast and effective relief from acute bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and Antimicrobial Properties: Elderberry's antiviral and antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying viral or bacterial infection causing bronchitis, reducing its severity and duration.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The flavonoids present in elderberries have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.
Expectorant Properties: Elderberry's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of bronchitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help soothe and calm respiratory inflammation and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Barks: Containing tannins and phenolic acids, barks have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce bronchial constriction and ease breathing difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in summer, ensuring at least 30 grams of dried plant material for processing.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method within 24 hours for optimal quality preservation.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, aiming for 1 gram per capsule.
Fill clean gelatin capsules with the powdered elder herb mixture, ensuring each capsule contains approximately 500 milligrams of herbal material.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to one year or until expiration date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain a concentrated extract of Rumex crispus, also known as curly dock, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm respiratory issues. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe, while its expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out mucus, allowing for a smoother recovery from acute bronchitis. Additionally, curly dock capsules may also help reduce coughing and congestion associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Curly dock capsules have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause acute bronchitis.
Expectorant and mucolytic effects: The saponins and other compounds present in curly dock may help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and reducing congestion.

Anti-inflammatory activity: Curly dock has been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and ease respiratory issues, which can help alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis such as chest tightness, wheezing, and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Curly Dock contain mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing inflammation in acute bronchitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the lungs, making them effective for treating acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried curly dock roots, ensuring freshness and absence of contamination.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 250 milligrams of ground curly dock root with 1000 milligrams of gelatin and 500 milligrams of water to create a filling mixture.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the prepared mixture and seal them securely, storing in an airtight container.
Store the finished capsules in a cool, dark place for up to six months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver daisy capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain a unique combination of herbs that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties in the capsules help reduce inflammation and congestion in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the expectorant properties loosen mucus, allowing for easy clearance from the lungs. This helps break down the infection and promote a faster recovery time, reducing the duration of acute bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Herbal silver daisy capsules have been shown to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which helps alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis such as coughing, wheezing, and chest tightness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in these capsules help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate lung damage and oxidative stress caused by acute bronchitis, promoting faster recovery.

Mucolytic activity: Herbal silver daisy capsules contain compounds that break down excess mucus in the airways, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion, coughing, and breathing difficulties associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Barks: The barks contain compounds that help to relieve coughs and ease congestion, making them a key component in treating acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ursinia anthemoides flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours or air drying for several weeks.
Grind 10g of dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered ursinia anthemoides with 100mg of silver nitrate and 50% glycerin in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with a mixture of 500mg herbal blend and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with acute bronchitis because of its expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The capsules contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated bronchial tubes, reducing inflammation and coughing fits. Additionally, mullein's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce congestion and open up airways, making it easier to breathe. As a natural remedy, mullein capsules can provide effective relief from acute bronchitis symptoms without harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Mullein's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of bronchitis.
Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant properties loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, helping to relieve congestion and coughs associated with acute bronchitis.
Antibacterial activity: Mullein has been shown to have antibacterial properties that help combat infections caused by bacteria such as Streptococcus pneumoniae, which can contribute to bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of mullein contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated respiratory tissues, making them an excellent choice for treating acute bronchitis.
Barks: Mullein bark is rich in saponins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and congestion in the lungs, helping to alleviate symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality verbascum thapsus roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dark place for at least seven days or use a dehydrator on low setting.
Grind 20 grams of dried verbascum thapsus root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 80 grams of empty gelatin capsules and fill them by spooning the mixture evenly among caps.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Loquat capsules helps with acute bronchitis because of their potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The loquat fruit contains a rich source of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins that work together to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the capsules help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs, promoting effective drainage and relief from congestion. This natural remedy can provide fast and effective relief from acute bronchitis symptoms, allowing patients to recover faster and feel better sooner.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Loquat capsules contain mucilage-rich compounds that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory property: The flavonoids and terpenes present in loquat capsules have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.

Antibacterial property: Loquat capsules contain compounds that exhibit antibacterial activity against pathogens commonly associated with acute bronchitis, such as Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae, which can help combat the infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe respiratory tract inflammation and reduce bronchial constriction.

Barks: Contain bioactive compounds like terpenes and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making them effective against acute bronchitis caused by viral or bacterial infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh eriobotrya japonica leaves and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent spoilage and enhance potency.
Grind 30g of dried eriobotrya japonica leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 60mg of gelatin in a small bowl, adding 10ml of boiling water to create a uniform mixture, allowing it to cool.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the herbal mixture, sealing them immediately to preserve potency and freshness for up to 2 years when stored in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mallow capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated airways and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. The mucilages present in the herb also help to lubricate and protect the mucous membranes lining the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel mucus and phlegm. Additionally, mallow's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and faster recovery from the infection.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mallow capsules contain mucilages, which soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by bronchitis. This helps to ease coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal mallow capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, making it easier for patients with acute bronchitis to breathe and cough up mucus.

Expectorant properties: Mallow capsules have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs, which is particularly helpful during an acute bronchitis episode when there's often a buildup of thick, sticky mucus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Mallow roots have been traditionally used to soothe respiratory issues and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for acute bronchitis treatment.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the mallow plant contain mucilages that help to calm irritated airways and ease coughing fits associated with acute bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried malva sylvestris flowers at peak blooming season.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle grinder.
Mix one part ground malva sylvestris with two parts potato starch to create a filling material.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal mixture per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways, relax bronchial smooth muscle, and promote the clearance of excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and expel phlegm. Additionally, milkweed's antioxidant properties can help protect lung tissues from further damage, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Milkweed contains saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, making it easier to expel mucus and promote effective breathing.

Phenolic acids: The phenolic acid content in milkweed may help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals in the lungs, which can contribute to chronic respiratory conditions like bronchitis.

Flavonoids: Milkweed's flavonoid compounds may have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, potentially helping to reduce the severity of acute bronchitis symptoms such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the milkweed plant have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including acute bronchitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.

Rhyzomes: Milkweed rhyzomes have been used in traditional medicine to relieve coughs and ease breathing difficulties associated with acute bronchitis, likely due to their expectorant and mucilage-rich compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh asclepias syriaca leaves and flowers at dawn on a sunny day in june.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 100g of dried asclepias syriaca with 50g of powdered marshmallow root and grind into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the herbal powder with 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of glycerin to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the herbal-honey-glycerin mixture per capsule, then seal and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterfly bush capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they provide a natural antispasmodic effect, relaxing the airway muscles and reducing coughing fits. The herbs contained in the capsules, such as passionflower and lemon balm, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the expectorant properties of the herbs help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, further alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.
remedy_properties: Thyme (Thymus vulgaris): Thyme's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties can help combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the lungs, which can exacerbate bronchial congestion.

Echinacea (Echinacea spp.): Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects may help boost the body's natural defense against respiratory infections, reducing the severity of bronchitis symptoms such as coughing and wheezing.

Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can help reduce inflammation and ease congestion in the lungs, making breathing easier for those with acute bronchitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
Barks: Contains anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in soothing airway congestion and reducing mucus production.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried buddleja davidii flowers, measuring 20g for the preparation.
Combine the dried flowers with 100ml of boiling water in a heatproof bowl.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes and then strain it through a cheesecloth.
Mix the resulting liquid with 50g of glycerin and 150ml of distilled water, stirring well until dissolved.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the herbal extract, sealing them promptly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain the essential oil lavandula angustifolia, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, relax bronchial muscles, and ease coughing spasms. As a result, English lavender capsules can provide quick relief from acute bronchitis symptoms such as wheezing, chest tightness, and difficulty breathing, allowing individuals to recover faster and more comfortably from this common respiratory condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English Lavender contains a compound called linalool, which has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of bronchitis.
Antispasmodic: The lavender's linalool content also acts as an antispasmodic, relaxing the airway muscles and reducing bronchial spasms, making it easier to breathe.

Expectorant: Lavender has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and other respiratory secretions, thereby relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to relax and calm the airways, making them ideal for reducing inflammation and congestion associated with acute bronchitis.
Flowers: Containing high amounts of linalool and linalyl acetate, these essential oils help to soothe and decongest the respiratory system, relieving coughs and bronchial spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried laurus nobilis leaves weighing exactly 250 grams.
Grind the leaves into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered laurus nobilis leaves with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container for 10 minutes.
Steep the mixture at 90 degrees celsius for exactly 60 minutes before straining it through cheesecloth to create a decoction.
Fill hard-shell gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams each of the prepared decoction and seal them for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb capsules helps with acute bronchitis because they contain sennosides, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. By reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs, herbal Chinese rhubarb capsules can provide quick relief from wheezing, coughing, and chest tightness associated with acute bronchitis. Additionally, the capsules' expectorant properties help to break down and remove excess mucus, making it easier for the body to expel it, thereby helping to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chinese Rhubarb contains compounds like rhein and emodin, which have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, alleviating symptoms of acute bronchitis.

Expectorant properties: The saponins present in Chinese Rhubarb can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion associated with acute bronchitis.

Antiviral properties: Some studies have shown that Chinese Rhubarb extracts exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, such as influenza and herpes simplex, which may help reduce the severity and duration of acute bronchitis caused by viral infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contains a high concentration of anthraquinones, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making it effective in relieving symptoms of acute bronchitis.
Rhyzomes: Rich in sennosides, which help to stimulate the contraction of the bronchial muscles, loosening mucus and facilitating its expulsion from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rhizoma rheum palmatum of good quality weigh out one hundred grams.
Crush the rhizoma rheum palmatum into fine powder using a mortar and pestle prepare for encapsulation.
Mix the powdered rhizoma rheum palmatum with 200 milliliters of distilled water soak for thirty minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl discard solids retain liquid.
Fill gelatin capsules with one half teaspoon of the resulting liquid extract and seal immediately.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for acute bronchitis are a natural remedy designed to alleviate symptoms of bronchial inflammation, congestion, and coughing. These capsules typically combine herbs such as ginger, turmeric, and peppermint that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to soothe the respiratory tract and ease breathing. Examples like BronchiClear and AirForce Plus contain a blend of herbs that help reduce inflammation, loosen mucus, and calm coughs. By using these herbal capsules, individuals with acute bronchitis can breathe easier, sleep better, and resume daily activities without discomfort or fatigue, improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acute bronchitis with herbal capsules is by combining them with a healthy lifestyle. Look for capsules containing herbs like turmeric, ginger, and eucalyptus, which help reduce inflammation and ease congestion. Take the capsules as directed and combine with steam inhalation, hydration, and rest to alleviate symptoms. This holistic approach can help soothe bronchial passages and promote recovery from acute bronchitis.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acute bronchitis that are treated with herbal capsules are chronic respiratory problems such as asthma, COPD, and bronchiectasis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like turmeric, ginger, and ashwagandha can help alleviate symptoms of coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Other conditions, including allergies, sinusitis, and upper respiratory tract infections, may also be treated with herbal supplements to promote immune system function and reduce inflammation.
